# VER-QUIC-CRT-0024 - QUIC CRT server revocation callback path verification

## Scope

Verify server-side standalone CertificateRevocationCheckMode delegation on the existing callback-driven ClientCertificateRequired path and its regression boundaries without widening broader policy promises.

## Requirements Verified

- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0126

## Verification Method

Execution, inspection, and regression evidence.

## Preconditions

- The Incursa.Quic library builds successfully on net10.0.
- The callback-driven ClientCertificateRequired floor from REQ-QUIC-CRT-0124 remains in place.
- The server runtime/bridge delegates standalone CertificateRevocationCheckMode-driven client-certificate evaluation to BCL X509Chain primitives when CertificateChainPolicy is absent.

## Procedure or Approach

- Run the focused server revocation requirement-home tests and the existing callback-driven ClientCertificateRequired tests.
- Run the current CRT, API, and INT guards that must remain unchanged, especially the mainstream client TLS and exact-pinning floors plus the transfer/retry contracts.
- Run the full `REQ_QUIC_CRT_` sweep.
- Run the repo-local SpecTrace validation and markdown rendering checks after the canonical JSON updates.

## Expected Result

- The server callback-driven ClientCertificateRequired path now honors standalone CertificateRevocationCheckMode through BCL X509Chain delegation when CertificateChainPolicy is absent.
- Mixed CertificateChainPolicy plus standalone revocation is rejected on this slice.
- The existing callback-driven floor, missing-client-certificate failure, server chain-policy floor, mainstream client TLS, exact pinning, transfer, retry, 0-RTT, and key update posture remain unchanged.

## Evidence

- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicServerConnectionOptionsValidator.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicListenerHost.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicConnectionRuntime.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTransportBridgeDriver.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/REQ-QUIC-CRT-0124.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/REQ-QUIC-CRT-0125.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/REQ-QUIC-CRT-0126.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/QUIC/REQ-QUIC-API-0005.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/QUIC/REQ-QUIC-API-0013.cs
- scripts/Validate-SpecTraceJson.ps1

## Status

This verification artifact covers the narrow server-side standalone CertificateRevocationCheckMode floor on the callback-driven ClientCertificateRequired path while leaving mixed policy handling unsupported.

## Related Artifacts

- SPEC-QUIC-CRT
- ARC-QUIC-CRT-0024
- WI-QUIC-CRT-0024

## Acceptance Criteria

- The server callback-driven ClientCertificateRequired path now accepts standalone CertificateRevocationCheckMode and passes a built X509Chain plus computed SslPolicyErrors into the existing callback when CertificateChainPolicy is absent.
- A mixed CertificateChainPolicy plus standalone revocation configuration is rejected on this slice.
- The existing callback-driven floor, missing-client-certificate failure, server chain-policy floor, mainstream client TLS mode, exact pinning floor, transfer contract, retry contract, and unsupported broader settings remain unchanged.
